findings.title: Reset tokens are generated with predictable non-CSPRNG values
Reset-token verification does not consume the token

reasoning: bcrypt uses cost 12 and bcrypt.compare; the reset limiter is mounted before its route; reset responses are generic; and no development bypass exists. However, reset token generation uses Date.now and Math.random instead of a CSPRNG. The repository contains no mutation that changes record.used after successful verification, so issued reset tokens are not established as single-use.
